info.name: GameMe API
info.description:
Full API collection for the GameMe gaming connection platform.

**Quick start:**
1. Run `POST /Seed/Create seed data` to load 120 test users.
2. Run `POST /Auth/Login` with any test user — the token is saved automatically.
3. All other requests use the saved token. No manual copy-paste needed.

Test credentials: `test10@test.com` / `1234` (or any `test10`–`test129@test.com`)

variable.description: JWT token — filled automatically after Login or Register
A target user ID to use in /users/{id} requests

item.name: Auth
item.description: Registration and login. No token required. The token is saved automatically after a successful login or register.

request.description: Register a new user. Returns a JWT token on success. The token is saved automatically to the `token` variable.
Login with an existing user. Returns a JWT token. The token is saved automatically to the `token` variable and used by all other requests.

item.name: Seed
item.description: Load or delete 120 fictitious test users. No token required.

request.description: Creates 120 test users (test10@test.com → test129@test.com, password: 1234). Idempotent — skips if 100+ users already exist.
Deletes all seeded users (test10@test.com → test129@test.com) and their connections and messages.

item.name: Me
item.description: Endpoints scoped to the currently authenticated user. All require a valid token.

request.description: Returns HTTP 302 redirecting to /api/users/{your-id}. Verifies the /me shortcut works.
Returns the authenticated user's own account data including email, nickname, and profile picture URL. Email is only returned here — never on /users/{id}.
Update nickname, email, or password. Only include the fields you want to change. Changing email requires `currentPassword` to be provided.

item.name: Get my bio (full — includes private fields)

request.description: Returns the full bio including private preference fields (preferredGenders, preferredAgeMin, preferredAgeMax, maxDistanceKm, latitude, longitude). These are never returned on /users/{id}/bio.

body.raw:
{
  "gender": "Male",
  "dateOfBirth": "2000-01-15",
  "timezone": "UTC+2",
  "timeRange": "16:00-22:00",
  "gamePreference": "Valorant, CS2",
  "gameGenrePreference": "FPS, Battle Royale",
  "lookingFor": "Play together",
  "platforms": "PC",
  "intensity": "7",
  "country": "Estonia",
  "city": "Tallinn",
  "latitude": 59.437,
  "longitude": 24.7536,
  "maxDistanceKm": 500,
  "preferredGenders": null,
  "preferredAgeMin": null,
  "preferredAgeMax": null
}

request.description: Partial update — only include fields you want to change. All fields are optional.

formdata.description: Select an image file (JPEG or PNG, max 5 MB)

request.description: Upload a profile picture. Select a file in the Body → form-data tab. Returns the updated user summary with the new picture URL.
Removes the profile picture. The avatar reverts to the default placeholder.
Returns a list of required profile fields that are still empty. Empty array = profile is complete and recommendations will be shown.

item.name: Users
item.description: Public user data endpoints. Returns 404 if the user does not exist OR if the caller is not allowed to view the profile (not recommended, no pending request, not connected).

request.description: Returns name, profile picture URL, and links to /profile and /bio. No email is included. Change the `userId` variable to test different users.
Returns the user's free-text 'About me' content.

item.name: Get user bio (public — no private preferences)

request.description: Returns public biographical data: gender, dateOfBirth, timezone, timeRange, games, genres, platforms, lookingFor, intensity, country, city. Does NOT include preferredGenders, preferredAgeMin/Max, maxDistanceKm, or GPS coordinates.
Should return 404. Verifies that the endpoint returns 404 for unknown IDs.

item.name: Recommendations
item.description: Returns a list of up to 10 recommended user IDs. Requires a complete profile.

request.description: Returns a plain JSON array of up to 10 user IDs — no user data included. Example: [4, 17, 23]. Returns an empty array or an error message if the profile is incomplete.

item.name: Connections
item.description: Manage connection requests and accepted connections.

request.description: Returns a plain JSON array of accepted connection user IDs. Example: [7, 12].
Send a connection request to the user with ID `{{userId}}`. The recipient is notified in real time via WebSocket.
Accept an incoming connection request from user `{{userId}}`. Both users are notified via WebSocket and can now chat.
Multi-purpose: rejects an incoming request, cancels an outgoing request, or removes an accepted connection. Both users are notified via WebSocket.
Blocks the user. The blocked user can no longer find you in recommendations or send connection requests.

item.name: Messages
item.description: Chat history between connected users. Paginated — 30 messages per page.

request.description: Returns the 30 most recent messages with user `{{userId}}`. Returns 403 if you are not connected with that user. Increment `page` to load older messages.
Returns messages 31–60 (older). If this returns an empty array, there are no more pages.
